expectations.text: Handles login, workspace creation, and profile setup since user didn't say they're already set up
expectations.evidence: Step 1 uses `clams init` which is interactive and blocks on stdin. Does not run login/workspaces create/profiles create independently.

expectations.text: Creates an onchain source (Electrum or Esplora) for the XPub connection even though user didn't mention one
expectations.evidence: Step 3: `clams onchain create --label electrum-public --kind Electrum --url ssl://electrum.blockstream.info:50002 --select`

expectations.text: Creates XPub connection with correct configuration including the user's xpub
expectations.evidence: Step 4: XPub with xpub, bech32, gap_limit 20, bitcoin network.

expectations.text: Creates LND connection with LNC transport and the user's pairing phrase
Creates Phoenix connection and imports the CSV file
expectations.evidence: Step 6a: `--kind Phoenix`, Step 6b: `clams connections import --label phoenix --input-format csv --file ~/Downloads/phoenix-export.csv`

expectations.text: Syncs connections (individually or with --all) after creating them
Runs clams journals process after syncing
Shows portfolio summary with USD fiat valuation (requires rate sync and fiat currency config)
expectations.evidence: Step 2 sets fiat to USD but no `clams rates sync` — rate cache may be empty, causing fiat values to be missing or zero. Step 10 runs portfolio-summary but without rate data the USD values would be incomplete.
